title: Share of adults who are obese vs. daily protein supply
subtitle: Obesity is defined as having a body-mass index (BMI) equal to, or greater than, 30. BMI is a person's weight (in kilograms) divided by their height (in meters) squared.
caption:
Share of adults who are obese vs. daily protein supply

Obesity is defined as having a body-mass index (BMI) equal to, or greater than, 30. BMI is a person's weight (in kilograms) divided by their height (in meters) squared.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between daily protein supply in grams per day per capita on the horizontal axis and the percentage of adults who are obese on the vertical axis for the year 2022. The data points are color-coded by region, distinguishing North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. A positive association is visible across the chart, where countries with higher protein supply generally show higher obesity rates. The lowest values appear in the bottom-left quadrant, where nations like Burundi, Somalia, and Bangladesh sit below 10% obesity with a protein supply under 60 grams. Moving toward the center, countries such as the World average, Algeria, and Colombia cluster in the 20% to 30% obesity range with protein supplies between 80 and 100 grams. The upper-right section contains the highest values, led by Tonga and Nauru, which both exceed 60% obesity with protein supplies over 110 grams. The United States and Qatar also occupy this high-protein, high-obesity zone, with obesity rates above 40%. European nations like Iceland, Ireland, and Norway display high protein availability exceeding 130 grams but maintain lower obesity rates between 20% and 30%, placing them to the far right of the distribution. Conversely, Iraq and Jordan show obesity rates near 40% despite having protein supplies closer to 60 or 70 grams, positioning them above the general trend line. The visual pattern shows that while obesity rates rise alongside protein supply for many nations, the slope of this increase varies by region and specific country context.
